## Who we are

Helsing is a defence AI company. Our mission is to protect our democracies. We aim to achieve technological leadership, so that open societies can continue to make sovereign decisions and control their ethical standards.

As democracies, we believe we have a special responsibility to be thoughtful about the development and deployment of powerful technologies like AI. We take this responsibility seriously.

We are an ambitious and committed team of engineers, AI specialists and customer-facing programme managers. We are looking for mission-driven people to join our European teams – and apply their skills to solve the most complex and impactful problems. We embrace an open and transparent culture that welcomes healthy debates on the use of technology in defence, its benefits, and its ethical implications.

## The role

Collaborating closely with our dynamic team of product designers and engineers, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the foundation of frontend at Helsing. **About Machine Learning Platform Engineering at Monzo**

The Machine Learning Platform team builds the systems that help teams across Monzo train, evaluate, deploy and serve ML models and AI features safely and reliably.

We work on backend services, Python libraries, model lifecycle tooling, evaluation workflows and low-latency serving systems. Our users are internal ML engineers, scientists and product teams building with ML and LLMs.

The work matters because machine learning powers many important decisions and experiences at Monzo, from fraud checks and credit decisions to customer operations. We help teams move faster while keeping production systems reliable, observable and safe.

This is a platform engineering role in the ML and AI space. We’re looking for someone who combines strong software engineering foundations with ML or AI context, and who enjoys building tools and systems for other engineers.

**🧰 What you'll be working with...**

* Go for backend services, platform APIs, and production systems
* Python for libraries, workflows, and tooling used by our ML engineers and scientists
* Feature platforms and data workflows using Chronon, Feast, and DataHub
* Model training pipelines and experiment tracking using Vertex AI and Comet
* AI observability, evaluation, and tracing using Langfuse and Bifrost
* AWS for real-time serving and online inference, GCP for batch compute and our BigQuery data warehouse.

(Please note direct experience with all of them is helpful but **not required** and our interview process can be completed in any language).

Our mission is to digitize the classic shopping stroll, turning it into a personalized, inspiring experience for every customer. Technology and data sit at the center of everything we build. # Engineer

**Job ID** 2022-5396
**Date posted** Mar. 17, 2026
**Location** Bengaluru, India 
**Category** Hardware Engineering
Apply

ARM CPU Verification Engineers have and develop specialist skills and knowledge and apply both hardware and software skills to the validation task. They work closely with the designers to understand various areas of the design, so that the features are thoroughly validated. This requires an insight both of the 'big picture' at the architectural level and of the detail at the implementation level.

## **Responsibilities:**

Technical Contribution as part of Project teams to deliver effective ARM CPU IPs

* Design and development of verification testbench and components needed for effective verification

* Effective communication skills, in terms of planning, reviews, status updates and meetings, as well as delivery against expectation
* Able to provide appropriate timescales for work assigned and assist in project planning by providing appropriate inputs to the specifications.
* Maintain a proficient knowledge of company processes in order to work constructively within given standards and methodologies
* Contribute towards efficiency improvement on verification tools and methodologies
* Support interpersonal development, mentor/coach team members for career development

## **Required Skills and Experience :**

* 2-4 years of proven experience in an IP/SOC product development environment
* Experience with Design Verification/validation standard methodologies such as Test Plan development, Testbench development and measurable execution thereof.
* Familiar with verification of processors based on Assembly language or C/C++
* Familiar with Microprocessor and/or SoC Architecture and micro Architecture, preferably ARM processors and ARM processor based systems

## **“Nice To Have” Skills and Experience :**

* Languages: Verilog, C/C++, Assembly language, Perl
* Verification Languages such as System Verilog.
* Strong understanding of CPU Architecture/micro-architectures.
* Experience of ARM based System Designs, and hierarchical memory system

There is a plenty of opportunity to craft the future of CPU verification, and your own growth and progression. This is an excellent opportunity to be part of the CPU design verification team and be part of Arm's key strategic objectives!

Sankt Gallen Metropolitan Area

Product – Engineering /

apply for this job

We're all about helping brands turn ideas into impact.

Frontify’s brand platform transforms how teams organize digital assets, collaborate on projects, and create engaging campaigns. Our people empower thousands of marketers and designers — including teams at Uber, Microsoft, Volkswagen, and Telefónica — to build engaging brands.

With headquarters in St. Gallen, Switzerland, and offices in London and New York City, we share a vibrant culture built on creativity, collaboration, inclusion, and joy. And we’re on the lookout for new team members to share our vision. If you’re ready for a brand-new adventure, keep reading!

**Your team**

Your new team is a group of motivated, collaborative, and innovative Engineers. Working on-site, hybrid, or fully remote, they come together to create and evolve a well-loved product but always keep room for new ideas and approaches. Outside of work, they’re parents, gamers, bookworms, adrenaline junkies, and so much more.

**Your mission**

Our team, Brand Fundamentals, owns the brand domain, and our mission is to ensure that the brand truth can be reliably consumed by humans, systems, and AI. We constantly raise the bar and build the tools that help our customers maintain consistency, while growing their brands.

You’ll lead the Brand Fundamentals teams and play a critical role in elevating our team's performance and capabilities. When leading both backend and frontend engineers in your team, you’ll need a deep understanding of how to architect, plan, and drive implementation in a SaaS product at scale. You will drive technical excellence and support the team in delivering impactful solutions.

### Your responsibilities

* Lead a software development team, mentor engineers, inspire high performance, and foster team cohesion.
* Mentor the engineers in your team and guide them to grow.
* Collaborate effectively with product managers, designers, and cross-functional stakeholders to drive outcomes and handle uncertainty.
* Contribute to product decisions, plan delivery cycles, and drive the team toward measurable, successful outcomes.
* Actively participate in hands-on development, working alongside engineers to design, implement, and review changes; take ownership of delivering high-quality software on time, proactively managing risks and dependencies.
* Architect scalable solutions, guide technical vision, and balance innovation with technical debt.

### Your story

* **You can work remotely from anywhere in Switzerland or on-site in our St. Gallen office – or even mix it up during the week!** Choose where to work from that works best for you.
* You’ve led software development teams for at least 2 years, mentoring engineers, inspiring high performance and fostering a cohesive team.

The Engineering team builds the software powering Veriff. We're cracking the online identity crisis in a scalable way, deploying excellence to keep Veriff on the cusp of innovation. It's a fast-paced market with many challenges.

We're on the lookout for talent to join our Customer Portal team as a Senior Software Engineer. The Customer Portal team builds and maintains our comprehensive self-serve platform, used by thousands of SaaS and Enterprise customers. This crucial product allows customers to procure, manage, and control their identity verification services in their daily operations. You'll own features end-to-end across our stack: a React/TypeScript front-end and the Node.js/TypeScript services behind it, where the full customer lifecycle comes together.

This is a frontend-leaning role with a solid grasp of backend architectures. You'll spend most of your time crafting high-quality customer-facing experiences, but you'll be equally comfortable dropping into the API layer, shipping a database change or debugging Production issues. You'll work closely with Product Managers and Designers to enable the best experience for our customers, and you'll help mentor and raise the bar across the team. Join us on this exciting adventure!

You’ll help us protect honest people online by:

  • Architecting a Multi-Product Engine: Shift from a single-product tool to a self-serve platform for 10+ services, building the billing and configuration logic that allows any customer to go live without manual intervention.
  • Balancing Enterprise & Scale: You’ll solve the tension between bespoke "whale" account requirements and a high-volume self-serve funnel, designing abstractions that satisfy complex SLAs without sacrificing platform speed.
  • Modernizing while Shipping: You will strategically rebuild 6-year-old legacy systems—specifically our high-traffic session list and decision profiles—ensuring our core infrastructure stays as modern as the new features we ship.
  • Engineering Cross-Team Alignment: As the entry point for Identity, Fraud, and Trust teams, you’ll coordinate how their products live in the portal, ensuring architectural consistency and code quality across the entire portfolio.
  • Amplifying Delivery with AI: You’ll help the team scale beyond its headcount by championing AI-enhanced workflows, turning "big ambitions" into reality through smarter automation and iterative, independent delivery.

title: Veriff
content:
All open positions # Senior Frontend Engineer (KYB) Function: Engineering Team: Engineering Location: Spain (Remote), Tallinn

The Engineering team at Veriff is solving complex, identity challenges at scale, driving how trust is established online, and continuously innovating in a fast-moving space. The KYB (Know Your Business) team focuses on verifying companies to ensure they are legitimate and trustworthy. This team owns the end-to-end KYB experience directly shaping how businesses onboard, operate, and grow. At Veriff, collaboration is key; it's a people-focused team where ideas are openly shared, and every voice contributes to the product. If you enjoy building thoughtful products, solving meaningful challenges, and working with systems at scale, this is an environment where you can make a significant impact.

We are seeking a Senior Frontend Engineer who combines strong product-thinking with deep engineering expertise. As a key member of the KYB team, you will collaborate closely with stakeholders to deliver the best possible experience for our customers. This role is not just about writing code; we are looking for an individual with exceptional vision, ready to lead the team to new levels of performance, tackle challenges proactively, and drive the team's success. Join us and help shape the future of online trust.

You’ll help us protect honest people online by:

  • Designing, developing, and testing software applications or systems using JavaScript, TypeScript and React
  • Producing high-quality, well-tested code with modern abstractions and frameworks
  • Supporting efforts to ensure cross-browser compatibility and responsive design
  • Staying up to date with frontend best practices and tools
  • Deepening your understanding of relevant product areas, codebase, and systems
  • Demonstrating proficiency in data analysis, programming, and software engineering
  • Collaborating with other teams, incorporating and sharing innovations
  • Designing systems with scalability, maintainability and product evolution in mind
  • Promoting quality, consistency, and alignment with established architectural principles via code reviews
  • Identifying and driving changes as needed for codebases, product area, and/or systems
  • Independently delivering new features to production as part of an iterative development process.
  • A keen interest in leveraging AI to enhance and streamline daily development workflows

You are the right future Veriffian for the job if you have:

# Prague

Ahoj! Welcome to Pipedrive Prague, a development center in the beautiful heart of Europe.

See open positions

## About us

Our Prague office is a dynamic engineering hub buzzing with diversity, culture and innovation. Home to 30+ talented teammates from more than ten countries, it’s a space designed to spark ideas and foster collaboration.

Movie-themed meeting rooms – including a Harry Potter fan favorite – make it a vibrant spot for team off-sites, attracting colleagues from across the globe.

Beyond work, we actively support Prague’s tech, data and HR communities, hosting regular meetups to share knowledge and inspire the next generation of talent.

## Our traditions

Our small, agile Prague team thrives on collaboration and innovation. A favorite tradition of ours is Lightning Talks, which began here in Prague to give people a safe, informal space to practice presenting. They’re optional, relaxed and always leave us feeling a little more inspired.

We take work-life balance seriously, with flexible hours that cater to night owls and early birds alike. Our office vibe is fun and welcoming, from foosball matches and ready-to-ride skateboards to carefully chosen coffee and tea, with a touch of Estonian flair from our slippers and sauna.

Connection is important at our Prague office, and we see it in the moments we share. Communal breakfasts, pickle-making and countless self-organized events capture the close-knit spirit that defines our team. We swap tips in our Prague-only Slack channels (prague-beauty, prague-investing and more), and when we clock out, you’ll find us belting out tunes during karaoke nights.

## Where you’ll find us

Our journey began in a co-working space, but in March 2020, we moved into a new office designed specifically for us. It’s located in a thriving Prague neighborhood – Karlín – and we’re sure we’re the first office in Prague with a sauna! We really enjoy the enchanting atmosphere here, which combines history and the pulse of modern business – complete with Harry Potter and Narnia-themed rooms that make work feel just a little more magical.

Our new home, Praga Studios, was recognized as one of the greenest buildings in the world. You can admire our beehives while drinking your coffee on the rooftop terrace with an amazing city view. Or, if you have green thumbs, try gardening right next to the building!

## Meet our team

At Pipedrive, I’ve felt how much people truly care about the product and design. As a designer, I know my voice matters, and I’m encouraged to share ideas and shape experiences that make a real impact. At the same time, being part of Pipedrive means working in a supportive and collaborative culture, surrounded by talented people who inspire you to grow every day.

# Tallinn

Welcome to Tallinn, where Pipedrive was born.

Come and join Pipedrive’s Tallinn team!

See open positions

## About us

Pipedrive was born in Tallinn. What started as a small startup in a shared office has grown into an international company with an entire building in Tallinn’s vibrant WoHo quarter. Today, Tallinn is home to our engineering and product hub, where 300+ team members from 30+ nationalities work together – many of them engineers.

Pipedrive is proud to be Estonia’s fifth unicorn and has consistently been recognized as one of the best places in the country to work since 2016, according to both the media and our employees. And we’re not just about jobs – at Pipedrive, you’ll have the chance to grow, learn and make a real impact by helping shape a product used by more than 100,000 companies worldwide.

## Our traditions

At Pipedrive, we love to celebrate the moments that matter – from birthdays and work anniversaries to promotions. One of our most beloved traditions is getting together for *Kringle*, a classic Estonian pastry, though you’ll also see pizza, donuts or pancakes make an appearance. To make sure no celebration goes unnoticed, we’ve got a kitchen-alert Slack channel to keep everyone in the loop.

Our biggest annual event is the Summer Garage – a full-day celebration that brings teams together to connect, unwind and enjoy live music performed by our very own band (and sometimes a guest artist or two).

We also host occasional hackathons and innovation accelerators to spark new ideas for improving the product and how we work together.

We believe in a balance of fun and focus. From Lightning Talks where anyone can practice their presentation skills with a topic they care about, to Sudoku tournaments and gaming challenges organized by teammates, there’s always something to get involved in.

We’re also proud to contribute to the wider community: Our team actively supports the local engineering, product and HR scenes in Estonia by hosting in-house meetups, participating in conferences and speaking at events.

Staying active outside of work is also a big part of our culture. Whether it's a friendly game of table tennis, a disc golf match or a padel session – one of the most popular sports among our Tallinn team – there's always fun going on.

## Where you’ll find us

You’ll find us in Tallinn’s modern WoHo district, where our custom-designed office makes it easy to feel at home. Each of our eight themed floors – park, nature, sports, IT, music, art, movies and culture – is fully immersive. Whether you’re jamming on the music floor’s instruments or exploring the art gallery, there’s something for everyone.

The heart of the office is our all-hands area, where people gather for a coffee or something else from our well stocked kitchens. Olá Lisboa! Say hello to our fun-loving office in the Portuguese capital.

See open positions

## About us

Pipedrive made a splash in Lisbon during Web Summit 2016, and our journey took off with the opening of our Lisbon office in 2017. After a few moves, we’re thrilled to call our vibrant Casal Ribeiro space home as of 2023.

With over 200 talented individuals from 25+ countries, Lisbon is one of our engineering hubs. But it’s our customer-facing teams that truly shine – they’re the heartbeat of our mission to keep users smiling. It’s a great place to work, collaborate and have fun while making a real impact!

## Our traditions

At our Lisbon office, we thrive on community and collaboration. We cherish lunch breaks to recharge, and as foodies, our official office cake is the delightful Bola de Berlin (bet you thought it was Pastel de Nata). The Lisbon office is also one of the few Pipedrive locations that goes all out for its birthday, a tradition the team takes very seriously.

With such a talented team of customer solutions experts, we host regular “One Day in Your Shoes” events to show off the incredible results they deliver for our customers. # Senior Software Engineer, ConsoleHelsinki, Uusimaa, Finland

Apply now

We’re a global team of over 400 people, working together to push the boundaries of open-source technology and multi-cloud solutions. Our vision is to help developers, builders, and creators bring their ideas to life with speed and simplicity, by providing a cloud data platform that makes open-source databases, search, streaming, and application infrastructure easily accessible to everyone.

### **The Role:**

We are hiring for a senior frontend-focused full-stack engineer to work on our Aiven Console. The Aiven Console enables our users to explore and control their data services via a web UI. As Aiven Console is often the customer’s first touchpoint with Aiven’s data platform and plays a big part in our product-led growth strategy, we want it to be an intuitive experience, while not forgetting the needs of our more experienced users.

We are looking for product-minded engineers who have a keen interest in the business and user behavior, are proactive with product development ideas and tradeoffs, and want to understand the “Why?” behind all things. As a product-minded engineer you communicate well with engineers and non-engineers alike and are interested in how other functions work.

As we are looking for a senior engineer, we expect you to raise the bar for the team through technical mentorship. We’d love you to show us how we can improve what we do. Our teams are proud of what we build and value curiosity, healthy debate, and exchange of ideas.

### **What You’ll Do:**

Here are some example areas you may work on:

* Own the Console implementation of platform-wide features, for example navigation, onboarding and the service creation flow.
* Working on our in-house design system that empowers our Console development
* Making sure our technical foundation stays in shape by driving technical improvements.
* Occasionally extend API functionality based on feature requirements

### **What we’re looking for:**

Our frontend is built with TypeScript, React and Redux, and our backend centers on Python and PostgreSQL. As such, to succeed in the role you have:

* Experience in leading the planning, implementation and rollout of a web-facing feature
* Excellent development skills in TypeScript and React
* Experience with Python and relational databases
* Fluent communication skills in English, written and verbal

If you think Aiven is the place for you and that our Values align with yours, send us your resume and we’ll get in touch!
